data.title: DevvStream 10-Q: $8,143 Revenue, $(0.98) EPS on Six‑Month Net Loss of $(3.94M)
data.description:
DevvStream reported minimal operating revenue of $8,143 for the six months ended in the latest Form 10‑Q, producing a six‑month net loss of $3,936,109 and a loss per share of $(0.98). Revenue remains nominal while operating expenses drove an operating loss of $4.42 million. The results reflect early-stage commercial activity alongside strategic shifts into digital treasury and crypto asset deployment.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.0081 million (six months revenue of $8,143).
  • Gross Profit: $(0.000514) million (six months gross loss of $(514)).
  • Operating Income: $(4.421) million (total operating expenses $4,420,560; operating loss).
  • Net Income: $(3.936) million (net loss for six months of $(3,936,109)).
  • Net Income Per Share: $(0.98) loss per share — basic and diluted for six months.

Business Highlights

  • Strategic Focus: Continued expansion in carbon markets and environmental asset monetization, including I‑RECs and nature‑ and tech‑based credits.
  • Revenue Trend: Modest revenue activity with limited sales; reported reduction in net loss versus prior periods indicating signs of stabilization.
  • Digital Treasury & Crypto: Launched a digital treasury strategy with purchases of BTC and SOL and initiated a staking program to diversify holdings and deploy capital.
  • Partnerships & M&A Activity: Entered merger/term sheet activities (Southern, XCF) and established a targeted $100 million SPV with Fayafi to scale project investments.
  • Operational Milestones: Completed De‑SPAC closing, achieved a NASDAQ listing, commenced token and warrant programs, and began active deployment of crypto assets.

Original SEC Filing: DevvStream Corp. [ DEVS ] - 10-Q - Mar. 13, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Auto industry groups urge Trump to keep Chinese autos out of US
data.description:
By David Shepardson

Major auto trade groups urged the Trump administration to keep Chinese carmakers out of the U.S., raising concerns ahead of President Donald Trump's planned meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping, according to a letter seen by Reuters.

The groups raised "serious concerns about China’s ongoing efforts to dominate global automotive manufacturing and to gain access to the U.S. market. These actions pose a direct threat to America’s global competitiveness, national security, and automotive industrial base."

A 2025 U.S. Commerce Department cybersecurity regulation effectively keeps nearly all Chinese vehicles out of the U.S. market and the five groups representing automakers, car dealers and parts manufacturers said it should be maintained.

The Chinese Embassy in Washington did not immediately comment.

"We also strongly urge the Administration to reject any attempt by Chinese manufacturers to circumvent these existing restrictions by establishing production facilities in the U.S.," said the letter dated Thursday from the Alliance for Automotive Innovation, National Automobile Dealers Association, American Automotive Policy Council and other groups. "The market distortions and risks to the auto industry in the U.S. are fundamentally the same whether these vehicles are imported or produced domestically."

In January, Trump said he was open to Chinese automakers building vehicles in the United States. "If they want to come in and build a plant and hire you and hire your friends and your neighbors, that’s great, I love that,” he told the Detroit Economic Club.

In December, the Alliance for Automotive Innovation, which represents General Motors, Ford, Toyota Motor, Volkswagen, Hyundai, Stellantis and other major automakers said "China poses a clear and present threat to the auto industry in the U.S" and urged Washington to prevent Chinese government-backed automakers and battery manufacturers from opening U.S. manufacturing plants.

data.title: Legacy Carmakers Retreat As EV Startups Launch Lower-Cost Models
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - Honda Motor Co. [HMC] has announced the cancellation of three planned electric vehicle projects for the U.S. market, contributing to the company's expected first annual loss since its public listing in 1957. This move highlights the growing pressure on traditional automakers as demand for electric vehicles slows in the U.S.

The company cites weaker-than-expected EV market expansion, reduced incentives, easing fossil fuel regulations, and tariff-related pressure on gasoline and hybrid profitability as factors behind this decision.

The shift away from these EV projects could cost Honda approximately 2.5 trillion yen or $15.7 billion over time, with a projected net loss of up to 630 billion yen or $3.94 billion for the current fiscal year.

Honda's announcement adds to a growing list of major automakers, including Ford Motor Company, General Motors, and Stellantis, that have taken multibillion-dollar charges related to delayed or canceled electric vehicle investments. Meanwhile, EV-focused startups like Rivian Automotive and Lucid Group are moving forward with more affordable product offerings.

Rivian recently opened orders for its R2 midsize SUV, starting at $57,990 for a performance version, with lower-priced trims scheduled for 2027. Lucid has also unveiled details of its upcoming Cosmos model, expected to cost less than $50,000 and offer 200 miles of range in just 14 minutes of charging.

Lucid plans to begin production of the Cosmos in Saudi Arabia this year, before ramping up U.S. manufacturing at its Arizona plant. Rivian's R2 is expected to be built in Normal, Illinois, and later supported by a future plant in Georgia.

Despite the weaker EV momentum in the U.S., several global automakers, including Toyota Motor Corporation, BMW, Volkswagen, and Hyundai Motor Company, continue to expand their electric offerings, particularly in Europe, Asia, and the Middle East.

data.title: MultiSensor AI Launches $60 Million At-The-Market Equity Program With Roth Capital and H.C. Wainwright
data.description:
MultiSensor AI unveiled a new at-the-market (ATM) equity program authorizing sales of up to $60 million of common stock through Roth Capital Partners and H.C. Wainwright & Co., with agent commissions up to 3.0%. Shares may be sold on Nasdaq or via other permitted methods, providing flexible access to capital. The company plans to use proceeds for working capital and general corporate needs. To facilitate the program, MultiSensor AI also executed an amendment to its existing Securities Purchase Agreement with certain investors to permit the ATM Offering.

Agreement 1: MultiSensor AI Launches $60 Million At-The-Market Equity Program With Roth Capital and H.C. Wainwright

  • Agreement type: At Market Issuance Sales Agreement for up to $60,000,000 of common stock
  • Counterparty: Roth Capital Partners; H.C. Wainwright & Co.
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 13 2026 / Mar 13 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise capital for working capital and general corporate purposes

Agreement 2: MultiSensor AI Amends Securities Purchase Agreement to Enable ATM Offering

  • Agreement type: Amendment No. 2 to Securities Purchase Agreement
  • Counterparty: Certain investors party to the Securities Purchase Agreement
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 12 2026 / Mar 12 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Permit the ATM equity offering

Original SEC Filing: MultiSensor AI Holdings, Inc. [ MSAI ] - 8-K - Mar. 13, 2026

data.title: Azitra Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On March 13, 2026, the company received notice from NYSE American that it is not in compliance with Section 1003(a)(iii) requiring at least $6.0 million in stockholders’ equity, as it reported $3.8 million and losses in its five most recent fiscal years. The company is operating under an accepted plan with a deadline of April 1, 2027 to regain compliance, and may face delisting proceedings if it fails to make sufficient progress or regain compliance by then.

Original SEC Filing: Azitra, Inc. [ AZTR ] - 8-K - Mar. 13, 2026

data.title: David Protein Bars Facing Lawsuit Over Calorie And Fat Discrepancies
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - David Protein, a company founded in 2024, is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that its protein bars contain significantly more fat and calories than stated on the product labels.

The lawsuit claims that some bars contain over 400% more fat and around 80% more calories than advertised.

The lawsuit, filed in January, states that bars labeled as 150 calories and 2 grams of fat were actually found to contain between 268 and 275 calories and 11 to 13.5 grams of fat through independent testing.

This exceeds the Food and Drug Administration's labeling tolerance rules, which generally allow nutrient values to exceed declared amounts by no more than 20%.

The company's founder, Peter Rahal, has rejected the claims, stating that the testing method used by the plaintiffs incorrectly counted calories from esterified propoxylated glycerol, a fat substitute used in the bars that is largely not digested by the body. Rahal argues that a bomb calorimeter measures total combustible energy, including nondigestible ingredients, rather than the actual calories absorbed by the body.

Rahal maintains that the use of EPG allows the bars to maintain lower calorie counts while preserving texture and taste, and that the company followed FDA guidance when calculating the label values. The independent testing cited in the lawsuit was conducted by Anresco Laboratories, which has declined to comment on the matter.

Nutrition experts note that EPG has been used for decades and can significantly lower the digestible fat content of products, though the distinction can be confusing for consumers reading nutrition labels.

Despite the lawsuit, the company, which has gained attention with backing from wellness figures like Andrew Huberman and Peter Attia, has stated that it intends to contest the claims.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Invesco Mortgage Capital Inc. March 2026 Dividend Announcement and February 28, 2026 Financial Update
data.description:
ATLANTA, March 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Invesco Mortgage Capital Inc. (the "Company") today announced that the Company declared a cash dividend of $0.12 per share of common stock for the month of March 2026. The dividend will be paid on April 14, 2026 to stockholders of record at the close of business on March 24, 2026, with an ex-dividend date of March 24, 2026.

Financial Highlights as of February 28, 2026

  • Total investment portfolio including TBAs of $7.3 billion
  • Unrestricted cash and unencumbered investments of $530.5 million
  • Total repurchase agreement borrowings of $5.4 billion
  • Estimated book value per common share(1) of $8.54
  • Debt-to-equity ratio of 6.0x and economic debt-to-equity ratio(2) of 7.3x

(1) Estimated book value per common share as of February 28, 2026 is
calculated as total stockholders' equity less the liquidation preference of
the Company's Series C Preferred Stock ($170.3 million), divided by total
common shares outstanding of 85.1 million.
(2) Economic debt-to-equity ratio is a non-GAAP financial measure and is
calculated as the ratio of total repurchase agreements and TBAs at implied
cost basis ($1.2 billion as of February 28, 2026) to total stockholders'
equity. Refer to the section titled 'Economic Debt-to-Equity Ratio' below for
additional information.

The Company is providing certain preliminary, unaudited month-end financial data as of February 28, 2026, including updates on the Company's book value, portfolio activity, leverage and liquidity. The information in this press release has been prepared by, and is the responsibility of, the Company's management. The Company's independent auditors have not audited, reviewed, examined, compiled nor applied agreed-upon procedures with respect to this information and, accordingly, they do not express an opinion or provide any form of assurance on the figures presented.

The preliminary metrics and estimates included in this press release are based on information that the Company believes to be reliable as of today's date and reflect management's judgment at this stage of the month-end closing process. This month-end update should not be viewed as a substitute for financial statements prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles ("U.S. GAAP") and is not necessarily indicative of results to be achieved in any future period. Additional items may be identified as part of the ongoing month-end and quarter-end closing processes, and such items could result in material revisions to the data presented in this press release. Accordingly, readers should not place undue reliance on the preliminary figures contained in this press release. The Company undertakes no obligation to update or revise the information contained herein, whether as a result of new information, subsequent events or otherwise.

Economic Debt-to-Equity Ratio

The Company presents an economic debt-to-equity ratio, a non-GAAP financial measure of leverage that considers the impact of the off-balance sheet financing of its investments in TBAs that are accounted for as derivative instruments under U.S. GAAP. The Company includes these types of TBAs at implied cost basis in its measure of leverage because a forward contract to acquire Agency RMBS in the TBA market carries similar risks to Agency RMBS purchased in the cash market and funded with on-balance sheet liabilities. Similarly, a contract for the forward sale of Agency RMBS has substantially the same effect as selling the underlying Agency RMBS and reducing the Company's on-balance sheet funding commitments. The Company believes that presenting its economic debt-to-equity ratio, when considered together with its U.S. GAAP financial measure of debt-to-equity ratio, provides information that is useful to investors in understanding how management evaluates at-risk leverage and gives investors a comparable statistic to those of other mortgage real estate investment trusts who also invest in TBAs and present a similar non-GAAP measure of leverage.

About Invesco Mortgage Capital Inc.

The Company is a real estate investment trust that primarily focuses on investing in, financing and managing mortgage-backed securities and other mortgage-related assets. The Company is externally managed and advised by Invesco Advisers, Inc., a registered investment adviser and an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Invesco Ltd., an independent global investment management firm.

Cautionary Notice Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

data.title: Aspen Aerogels 2025 10-K: Revenue $271.1M, EPS $(4.73)
data.description:
Aspen Aerogels reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $271.1 million and a net loss per share of $(4.73), driven by a steep decline in thermal barrier sales and significant impairment and restructuring charges. Gross profit fell to $46.0 million (17% of revenue) while operating loss widened due to those charges, producing a net loss of $389.6 million for the year. The results reflect a material shift in product mix and operational disruptions that affected capacity and costs.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $271.1 million, down 40% year-over-year (2025 vs 2024) due to lower thermal barrier volumes.
  • Gross Profit: $46.0 million, representing 17% of revenue (down from $182.9 million and 40% margin in 2024).
  • Income (loss) from operations: $(378.2) million, reflecting impairment and restructuring charges in 2025.
  • Net income (loss): $(389.6) million, compared with $13.4 million net income in 2024.
  • Net income (loss) per share: $(4.73), basic and diluted.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Thermal barrier sales declined 45% in 2025, driving the 40% total revenue decline and increasing reliance on the energy industrial mix.
  • Customer concentration: Top 10 customers accounted for approximately 84% of revenue, with a single large OEM customer (GM) representing about 59% of 2025 revenue.
  • Manufacturing adjustments: Converted the East Providence facility to support thermal barrier growth and relied on external fabrication in China and Mexico to flex capacity.
  • Operational disruptions & responses: Statesboro plant operations were halted and the site was impaired; a facility fire damaged an emissions unit; the company implemented headcount reductions and demobilization to manage costs.
  • Product momentum: Continued focus on PyroThin thermal barriers and Pyrogel/Cryogel industrial insulations with multi-year OEM production contracts supporting future production plans.

Original SEC Filing: ASPEN AEROGELS INC [ ASPN ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Ernexa Therapeutics 2025 10-K: $0.0M Revenue, Net Loss $(14.08)M
data.description:
Ernexa Therapeutics reported $0.0 million in revenue for the year ended December 31, 2025, compared with $0.582 million in 2024, and posted a net loss of $(14.084) million for 2025 (improved from a $(44.539) million loss in 2024). Gross profit was $0.486 million in 2024 and $0 in 2025 due to no revenue recognition in the current year. The company cited financing activity in 2025 and 2026 to support its preclinical development while reducing operating expenses to extend runway.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.0 million for year ended Dec 31, 2025 (was $0.582 million in 2024).
  • Gross Profit: $0.486 million recorded in 2024; $0 in 2025 due to no revenue.
  • Operating Income (Loss): Loss from operations of $(9.319) million in 2025, improved from $(15.674) million in 2024.
  • Net Income (Net Loss): Net loss of $(14.084) million in 2025, improved from $(44.539) million in 2024.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not presented explicitly in Part 7/8 of the filing and therefore omitted.

Business Highlights

  • Clinical and R&D Focus: Continued preclinical-stage development of synthetic allogeneic induced mesenchymal stem cell (iMSC) therapies targeting oncology and autoimmune indications.
  • Revenue & Contract Shifts: Company recognized no revenue in 2025 after prior cell-line customization work was reassigned to Factor Bioscience, altering the expected future revenue flow.
  • Capital Raises & Runway: Completed a private placement in 2025 and a public offering in 2026 to fund development activities; cash constraints create near-term operational uncertainty.
  • Cost and Facility Optimization: Reduced general and administrative and R&D expenses driven by sublease termination, lower occupancy costs, reduced professional fees, and targeted consulting spend.
  • Regulatory & Corporate Actions: Implemented corporate governance changes including an increase in authorized shares, actions by written consent, and a 1-for-15 reverse stock split to support financing flexibility.

Original SEC Filing: Ernexa Therapeutics Inc. [ ERNA ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Cardio Diagnostics 2025 10-K: Revenue $14,825, EPS $(3.71)
data.description:
Cardio Diagnostics reported revenue of $14,825 for the fiscal year 2025 and a net loss of $(6,498,167), or $(3.71) per share (basic and diluted, retroactively restated for a 1-for-30 reverse split). Revenue declined following the conclusion of a key testing pilot, while net loss improved versus 2024 primarily due to lower stock-based compensation expense. Operating losses narrowed to $(6,484,078) as G&A stock compensation decreased year over year.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $14,825 (decline from $34,890 in 2024 due to conclusion of key testing initiative)
  • Operating Income: $(6,484,078) (loss from operations for 2025 vs $(8,365,877) in 2024; reduced G&A stock compensation lowered operating loss)
  • Net Income: $(6,498,167) (net loss improved $1,885,286 vs 2024 primarily from lower stock-based comp expense)
  • Net Income Per Share: $(3.71) (basic and fully diluted loss per share, retroactively restated for 1-for-30 reverse split)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue declined to $14.8K in 2025 from $34.9K in 2024, reflecting the conclusion of a pilot program and a slower-than-expected ramp with new, smaller provider customers.
  • Channel & GTM Shift: Pivoted go-to-market from telemedicine/concierge channels toward broader partners, including health systems, employers, payors, life insurers and international partners.
  • Product & Brand Momentum: Launched new offerings including Epi+Gen CHD, PrecisionCHD, CardioInnovate360 (research-use-only), and the HeartRisk platform, positioning the company toward epigenetics-focused cardiovascular diagnostics.
  • Operational Milestones: Established CLIA laboratory setup and completed first international expansion to India; commercialization activity continues but provider onboarding cycles remain longer.
  • Evidence & Reimbursement Focus: Prioritizing R&D, health economics evidence generation, leveraging CPT PLA code and advancing payor reimbursement efforts to support adoption.

Original SEC Filing: Cardio Diagnostics Holdings, Inc. [ CDIO ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: California BanCorp 10-K: Net interest income $169.09M; EPS $1.93
data.description:
California BanCorp reported strong 2025 results driven by higher net interest income and a reversal of credit loss provisions, producing net interest income of $169.092 million and diluted EPS of $1.93. Net income rose to $63.058 million, benefiting from merger-related asset growth and an $(8.823) million reversal of the provision for credit losses. The company cited improved noninterest income and operational integration following its all-stock merger with CALB.

Financial Highlights

  • Net interest income: $169.092 million (primary revenue source), up $46.1 million year-over-year — attributed to the merger and higher average earning assets.
  • Noninterest income: $11.085 million, up $6.3 million year-over-year driven by BOLI, fees and other equity income.
  • Net income: $63.058 million, up $57.625 million year-over-year — improvement driven by higher net interest income and reversal of credit loss provision.
  • Diluted net income per share: $1.93, compared with $0.22 in the prior year.
  • (Reversal of) provision for credit losses: $(8.823) million, improved from a $21.690 million provision in the prior year, supporting earnings and efficiency.

Business Highlights

  • Merger expansion: Completed an all-stock merger with CALB (effective July 31, 2024), expanding the franchise into Northern California and adding branches and offices; integration activities and staff increases followed.
  • Revenue momentum and deposit mix: Asset growth and accretion from the merger materially boosted net interest income and pre-tax, pre-provision income; transactional and digital deposits increased with growth in NOW and money market balances and utilization of ICS/CDARS affecting deposit composition.
  • Credit and portfolio derisking: Executed a derisking strategy that reduced criticized loans and nonperforming assets, contributing to reversals in credit loss provisions across 2025.
  • Operational investments: Integrated operations post-merger, increased FTEs, expanded treasury and servicing capabilities, and completed dispositions of OREO and resolution of litigation items.

Original SEC Filing: California BanCorp \ CA [ BCAL ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Bayview Acquisition 10-K: Net income $1.753M in 2024, trust interest funds operating costs
data.description:
Bayview Acquisition reported net income driven by interest earned on cash and trust investments, with $1.753 million for the year ended 2024 and $0.203 million in the comparative period, reflecting financing and formation-related activity rather than operating revenues. The company has no operating revenues as it remains a blank-check acquisition vehicle and the trust interest income primarily offset public-company costs. Net income per share figures were not presented in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income (recent period): $1.753 million (2024), primarily interest income on trust and bank less formation and operating costs.
  • Net Income (prior period): $0.203 million, primarily interest income on trust and bank offset by formation and operating costs.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not presented in Part II Item 7/Item 8 and omitted per filing instructions.

Business Highlights

  • IPO and Capital Formation: Completed an initial public offering and related private placement raising $62.325 million, with proceeds placed in a trust to finance an initial business combination.
  • No Operating Revenues: Operates as a special purpose acquisition company (blank-check), with activities limited to formation, IPO preparation, and searching for potential acquisition targets.
  • Trust Investment Income: Interest earned on trust and cash equivalents generated the reported net income and is used to fund public-company expenses and taxes.
  • Deal-Readiness Activities: Incurring legal, accounting, due diligence and listing expenses while evaluating and negotiating potential targets in anticipation of a business combination.

Original SEC Filing: Bayview Acquisition Corp [ BAYA ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2026

data.title: Annovis Bio 10-K: Net loss $28.9M; Cash $19.5M, runway into Q3 2026
data.description:
Annovis Bio reported a net loss of $28.9 million for the year, wider than the $24.6 million loss in 2024, driven by higher clinical spending and operating expenses. The company held $19.5 million in cash and cash equivalents at year-end, which management indicates is insufficient to fund operations for more than 12 months and gives a runway into the third quarter of 2026. Operating losses reflect elevated R&D investment related to a pivotal Phase 3 Alzheimer’s disease trial and ongoing clinical programs.

Financial Highlights

  • Net loss: $28.9 million (increase of $4.3 million vs. $24.6 million in 2024)
  • Total operating expense: $29.7 million (up $3.0 million vs. $26.7 million in 2024)
  • Research and development: $25.2 million (up $5.2 million vs. $20.0 million in 2024)
  • General and administrative: $4.5 million (down $2.2 million vs. $6.7 million in 2024)
  • Cash and cash equivalents: $19.5 million; cash runway into Q3 2026 and insufficient to fund operations for more than 12 months

Business Highlights

  • Clinical programs: Initiated an FDA-cleared pivotal Phase 3 Alzheimer’s disease trial enrolling 760 patients in February 2025.
  • Extension study: Began a 36-month open-label Parkinson’s disease extension in January 2026 to continue assessing longer-term effects.
  • Efficacy signals & biomarkers: Prior Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s trials showed dose-dependent cognitive and motor improvements with supporting CSF and plasma biomarker validation (including p‑tau217 and MRI measures).
  • R&D operations: Year-over-year R&D spending rose due to Phase 3 trial execution; the company expects elevated clinical activity into 2026 and continues to rely on third-party CROs and CMOs.
  • Capital strategy: Completed multiple equity offerings and ATM sales in 2024–2025 to provide funding, but additional fundraising is critical to sustain ongoing trials and operations.

Original SEC Filing: Annovis Bio, Inc. [ ANVS ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2026

data.title: Simeon Palios Discloses Investment at Diana Shipping Inc. with 5.7% Stake
data.description:
Simeon Palios disclosed a 5.7% stake in DIANA SHIPPING INC., reporting indirect ownership through Taracan Investments S.A. and Limon Compania Financiera S.A. The Schedule 13D was filed on Mar. 13, 2026, and reflects a recent grant of 1,694,227 shares to Taracan on Feb. 25, 2026. As a director of Diana Shipping, Palios now holds a position that could shape strategy and governance.

Investor Intent

Palios holds the shares for investment but keeps options open. He notes that he "may act in concert with any other shareholders... for a common purpose" and remains "in contact with members of management and the Board... regarding alternatives... to increase shareholder value." He may buy more shares, receive equity grants, or sell holdings as he evaluates opportunities.

Investor's Background

Simeon Palios is a Greek shipping executive and longtime leader associated with Diana Shipping, where he has served as Chairman and CEO. He focuses on dry bulk shipping and capital allocation tied to long-term fleet management and market cycles. Palios often aligns ownership with governance roles to influence operational and financial strategy.

data.title: Warsh Fed nomination stays on hold while legal battle over Powell continues
data.description:
Key points:
  • Pirro's probe of Powell seen as attack on Fed independence
  • Senator Tillis blocks Warsh nomination until probe ends
  • Powell chair term ends May 15, pending Warsh confirmation

By Howard Schneider

President Donald Trump's nomination of Kevin Warsh to become Federal Reserve chair remains stalled for now in a continued clash between a key Republican senator and a Trump-appointed prosecutor, even as current Chair Jerome Powell's term heads into its final weeks.

Jeanine Pirro, the U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia, said on Friday she will continue her criminal probe of Powell involving Fed building renovations after a judge quashed grand jury subpoenas and called the investigation a pretext to pressure the central bank for lower interest rates, as Trump has done.

Trump has engaged in unprecedented efforts to sway central bank policymakers and overhaul the Fed's governing body.

While Pirro cast her decision to appeal U.S. District Judge James Boasberg's ruling as a defense of the grand jury system, the practical effect is also to keep Warsh's nomination on hold.

Republican U.S. Senator Thom Tillis said he will not vote to advance any of Trump's Fed nominees until the probe of Powell is closed. Tillis, a member of the Senate committee that would vet Warsh's nomination, has clashed with Trump since deciding not to run for reelection to the Senate.

"This ruling confirms just how weak and frivolous the criminal investigation of Chairman Powell is and it is nothing more than a failed attack on Fed independence," Tillis wrote on X after the judge's ruling. "We all know how this is going to end and the D.C. U.S. Attorney's Office should save itself further embarrassment. Appealing the ruling will only delay the confirmation of Kevin Warsh as the next Fed Chair."

The judge blocked the subpoenas after finding "no evidence whatsoever" of wrongdoing by Powell.

The Fed was designed by Congress to insulate its officials from political influence in setting interest rates, an approach followed to a greater or lesser degree by all top central banks globally.

Pirro, in a voluble press conference, said she has no plans to back off, and brushed aside questions about her decision potentially keeping Warsh from joining the Fed and potentially allowing Powell to continue chairing rate setting meetings beyond the end of his four-year term leading the central bank.

"I don't care what they say, I have a job. I have the ability to go into a grand jury. There are questions that the American public and people in D.C. are entitled to know," Pirro said. "This decision will be appealed by the United States Department of Justice."

Powell has called the probe a pretext to pressure the Fed on interest rates.

Her decision now ties Warsh's fate to the speed and outcome of the appeals process.

Powell's term as chair ends on May 15, with two more meetings of the central bank between now and then. His term as a Fed governor continues, however, and if Warsh has not been confirmed by the Senate in time for the Fed's June 16-17 Powell would continue leading the meetings of the rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee.

It would be an ironic twist, with the actions of one member of the Trump administration upending the president's long-sought goal of replacing Powell, but one that becomes increasingly possible as time passes.

"Judge Boasberg makes clear what everyone knew all along: This was a blatant abuse of power," said Kathryn Judge, a Columbia University law school professor who has followed issues around the Fed's legal structure and protections. "Pirro at this point seems to be far more of an impediment than an aid to the president's efforts to install his preferred team at the Fed. Any effort to appeal seems likely to fail and will only delay Senate hearings for Warsh."

data.title: Fitch revises Rwanda's outlook to 'stable' on easing regional security tensions
data.description:
Credit ratings agency Fitch revised Rwanda's outlook to "stable" from "negative" on Friday, citing reduced uncertainty over access to external funding and improved diplomatic engagement that has eased regional security tensions.

The agency affirmed its ratings at "B+".

data.title: Preliminary Proxy Statement and Irish Statutory Accounts
data.description:
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 13, 2026--

CRH , the leading provider of building materials, today filed a Preliminary Proxy Statement for the 2026 Annual General Meeting on Schedule 14A with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC").

The Preliminary Proxy Statement is filed with the SEC in accordance with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The Preliminary Proxy Statement is available to view on the SEC's website at https://www.sec.gov and the Company's website at https://www.crh.com.

The financial statements of the Company for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025 prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) as adopted by the European Union (the "Irish Statutory Accounts") were also published by the Company today and are also available to view on the Company's website at https://www.crh.com.

The Preliminary Proxy Statement and the Irish Statutory Accounts have also been submitted to the U.K. National Storage Mechanism and will shortly be available for inspection at https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism.

data.title: Amazon Raises $36.8B in Senior Note Sales Amid Data-Center Buildout
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Amazon raised $36.81 billion through a senior note sale.

The company closed sales of senior notes with due dates between 2028 and 2076 through a public offering on Friday, it said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission. It sold the notes for $36.9 billion, netting $36.81 billion after deducting underwriting discounts and offering expenses.

The substantial borrowing comes as Amazon is planning for $200 billion in capital expenditures this year, largely financing Amazon Web Services expansion as the company looks to beef up its data center business.

The note sale was registered on Feb. 6, a day after Amazon released its fourth-quarter financial results and announced the capital expenditure target.

JPMorgan Securities, Citigroup Global Markets, Goldman Sachs, and HSBC Securities acted as managers of the underwriters.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Office Properties Income Trust files Chapter 11, furnishes audited 2025 financials and discloses $310M offer for March 2029 notes
data.description:
Office Properties Income Trust delivered audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended Dec. 31, 2025 and disclosed Chapter 11 restructuring negotiations including a $310 million settlement proposal for March 2029 notes.

Key Highlights:

  • Filed Chapter 11 on Oct. 30, 2025 to implement a court‑supervised restructuring under an RSA; cases jointly administered in SDTX.
  • Furnished audited consolidated financial statements for year ended Dec. 31, 2025 (Exhibit 99.1) to certain debtholders to satisfy covenants.
  • Deloitte issued unqualified opinions on the 2025 financials and internal control, but noted going concern and bankruptcy explanatory paragraphs.
  • Disclosed negotiations with March 2029 ad hoc group: creditors asserted claims ≥ $321M; company proposed settlement of $310M inclusive of advisor fees.
  • Cautions shareholders trading common shares is highly speculative; common shares no longer listed on Nasdaq and recoveries in Chapter 11 are uncertain.

Original SEC Filing: OFFICE PROPERTIES INCOME TRUST [ OPI ] - 8-K - Mar. 13, 2026
